rulings.Case Title: GARRITT BLANZ ET AL VS. ROBERT C HINCKLEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement And For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Terms Of Settlement On Record [Ccp 664.6]
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 08, 2022 line 1. DEFENDANT ROBERT HINCKLEY, CHRISTINE BARTLETT HINCKLEY Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement And For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Terms Of Settlement On Record [Ccp 664.6] is continued to February 18, 2022.=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ANNE SCOTT FAMILY TRUST ET AL VS. ANNA THADDEA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 08, 2022 line 6. DEFENDANT ANNA THADDEA MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND Hearing Required. rulings.Case Title: EQUITY RESIDENTIAL MANAGEMENT, LLC VS. KEITH WAGNER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 08, 2022 line 7. DEFENDANT KEITH WAGNER DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, complaint contains allegations stating a cause of action for unlawful detainer based on non-payment of rent. Any factual disputes (e.g, return of Financial Distress Declaration, etc) are not properly raised on a demurrer. Plaintiff shall file and serve an amended form UD-101 correcting the allegations ONLY in paragraphs 7d. Defendant shall have 5 business days from service of amended UD-101 to answer. The Court notes that Defendant failed to comply with SFLR 2.7B. Failure to do so in the future will result in motions being taken off calendar. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SHU RU LEI VS. XI LING LIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 08, 2022 line 11. DEFENDANT WEN WEN XU, MUDE XU MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is DENIED. Occupants may file their Pre-Judgment Claim of Right to Possession no later than February 10, 2022 if they assert any possessory rights and the nature of those rights (tenants, subtenants, etc). Plaintiff shall amend the complaint thereafter and name the claimants as Defendants. Claimants shall have 5 business days thereafter to respond. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
